documents.t: The canonical contributing guide is CONTRIBUTING.md in the repo. This page is a quick summary; please read that one for the full policy.

homelab-nut is a CLI + TUI for wiring up Network UPS Tools (NUT) across a homelab — monitor your UPS, push notifications when power events happen, and gracefully shut everything down when the battery runs low.
The canonical roadmap lives at ROADMAP.md in the repo. Live status of open work is at TODOS.md (regenerated from GitHub Issues with make todos).
Modern CLI + TUI for Network UPS Tools (NUT)

homelab-nut sets up and operates a NUT-based UPS fleet from your laptop. Run with no arguments to open the interactive TUI. Subcommands like init, plan, apply, status are exposed for scripted use. See https://github.com/rtorcato/homelab-nut/blob/main/ROADMAP.md homelab-nut [flags]
-h, --help help for homelab-nut -i, --inventory string path to the inventory YAML file (default: ./homelab-nut.yaml if present, else ~/homelab-nut.yaml)

homelab-nut apply - Plan + execute changes on each host over SSH homelab-nut backup - Snapshot a host's NUT + homelab-nut config to a local tarball homelab-nut detect - Scan nut-server host(s) for connected UPS hardware (nut-scanner) homelab-nut init - Generate homelab-nut.yaml interactively homelab-nut inventory - Inspect and edit the homelab-nut inventory homelab-nut logs - Tail a host's systemd journal over SSH homelab-nut plan - Preview what apply would change on each host (or JSON with -o json) homelab-nut shutdown - Operate the fleet's shutdown chain homelab-nut status - Poll each nut-server host for live UPS state homelab-nut uninstall - Remove homelab-nut's services + binaries from a host (the inverse of apply) homelab-nut version - Print the version, commit, and build date (or JSON with -o json) Auto generated by spf13/cobra on 24-Jun-2026​
Plan + execute changes on each host over SSH

Loads the inventory, plans what would change on each host, prompts for confirmation (unless --auto-approve), then executes Apply for each role with a non-empty diff. Streams each role's output prefixed with [host/role] so concurrent fleet output stays attributable. homelab-nut apply [flags]

-y, --auto-approve skip the y/N confirmation prompt before applying --concurrency int max hosts to apply against in parallel (0 = unlimited) -h, --help help for apply --host string apply only this host (the full inventory is still used to resolve cross-host roles) -o, --output string output format: text (human) | json (machine-readable for AI/scripts) (default "text")
-i, --inventory string path to the inventory YAML file (default: ./homelab-nut.yaml if present, else ~/homelab-nut.yaml)
homelab-nut - Modern CLI + TUI for Network UPS Tools (NUT) Auto generated by spf13/cobra on 24-Jun-2026​
Snapshot a host's NUT + homelab-nut config to a local tarball

Pulls the files homelab-nut cares about off a target over SSH into a local gzip tarball: /etc/nut configs, the custom systemd units, a MANIFEST (NUT / exporter / kernel versions), and a snapshot of the host's inventory entry. Read-only on the target — no --auto-approve needed. Secrets (upsd.users password hashes, the exporter's scrape credentials) are excluded by default so the tarball is safe to share; pass --include-secrets for a true backup-and-restore capture. Default path: ./backups/-.tar.gz homelab-nut backup [host] [flags]
homelab-nut backup pi-rack homelab-nut backup --all --output /mnt/backups homelab-nut backup pi-rack --include-secrets -o json

--all back up every host in the inventory (required if no host is given) --concurrency int max hosts to back up in parallel (0 = unlimited) -h, --help help for backup --include-secrets include upsd.users + /etc/default/nut-exporter (sensitive) -O, --out string destination directory, or a .tar.gz file path for a single host (default: ./backups) -o, --output string output format: text (human) | json (machine-readable for AI/scripts) (default "text")
-i, --inventory string path to the inventory YAML file (default: ./homelab-nut.yaml if present, else ~/homelab-nut.yaml)
homelab-nut - Modern CLI + TUI for Network UPS Tools (NUT) Auto generated by spf13/cobra on 24-Jun-2026​
Scan nut-server host(s) for connected UPS hardware (nut-scanner)

Connects over SSH to a nut-server host and runs nut-scanner to detect the connected UPS and the driver NUT should use for it. With no argument, every host with the nut-server role is scanned. By default detect is read-only: it reports what's connected and the recommended driver. Pass --write to persist the detected driver into the inventory (only when exactly one UPS is found, to avoid guessing). nut-scanner ships with the NUT package, which 'homelab-nut apply' installs — so a brand-new host must be applied once before detect can see anything. With -o json, output is a JSON array suitable for AI agents and scripts. homelab-nut detect [host] [flags]

-h, --help help for detect -o, --output string output format: text (human) | json (machine-readable for AI/scripts) (default "text") --write persist the detected driver into the inventory (single-UPS hosts only)
-i, --inventory string path to the inventory YAML file (default: ./homelab-nut.yaml if present, else ~/homelab-nut.yaml)
homelab-nut - Modern CLI + TUI for Network UPS Tools (NUT) Auto generated by spf13/cobra on 24-Jun-2026​

Walks through a guided form, then writes the resulting inventory. With no --inventory/-i flag it writes ./homelab-nut.yaml when that file already exists, otherwise ~/homelab-nut.yaml. If an inventory already exists, you'll be asked whether to overwrite it. This subcommand and the TUI's empty-state 'i' shortcut run the same forms — use whichever fits your context. homelab-nut init [flags]
-h, --help help for init --no-apply write the inventory but don't apply it (generate only — run apply later)
-i, --inventory string path to the inventory YAML file (default: ./homelab-nut.yaml if present, else ~/homelab-nut.yaml)
homelab-nut - Modern CLI + TUI for Network UPS Tools (NUT) Auto generated by spf13/cobra on 24-Jun-2026​
Inspect and edit the homelab-nut inventory

The inventory is a YAML file describing the hosts in your homelab, their roles, and the UPS attachments. With no --inventory/-i flag, homelab-nut uses ./homelab-nut.yaml if present, otherwise ~/homelab-nut.yaml. Subcommands let you list hosts, validate the file, show a single host, or open the file in your $EDITOR (re-validating on save).
-i, --inventory string path to the inventory YAML file (default: ./homelab-nut.yaml if present, else ~/homelab-nut.yaml)

homelab-nut - Modern CLI + TUI for Network UPS Tools (NUT) homelab-nut inventory edit - Open the inventory in $EDITOR and re-validate on save homelab-nut inventory list - Print a table of hosts (or JSON with -o json) homelab-nut inventory show - Show details for one host (or JSON with -o json) homelab-nut inventory validate - Check the inventory against the schema and rules Auto generated by spf13/cobra on 24-Jun-2026​
Open the inventory in $EDITOR and re-validate on save homelab-nut inventory edit [flags]
-i, --inventory string path to the inventory YAML file (default: ./homelab-nut.yaml if present, else ~/homelab-nut.yaml)
homelab-nut inventory - Inspect and edit the homelab-nut inventory Auto generated by spf13/cobra on 24-Jun-2026​

-i, --inventory string path to the inventory YAML file (default: ./homelab-nut.yaml if present, else ~/homelab-nut.yaml)
homelab-nut inventory - Inspect and edit the homelab-nut inventory Auto generated by spf13/cobra on 24-Jun-2026​

-h, --help help for show -o, --output string output format: text (human) | json (machine-readable for AI/scripts) (default "text")
-i, --inventory string path to the inventory YAML file (default: ./homelab-nut.yaml if present, else ~/homelab-nut.yaml)

Check the inventory against the schema and rules homelab-nut inventory validate [flags]

Tail a host's systemd journal over SSH

Connects to a host over SSH and streams its systemd journal for a NUT unit — by default the battery-shutdown daemon (ups-battery-shutdown), the service you most often need when a shutdown misbehaves. Pass --unit to read any other unit (e.g. nut-server, nut-monitor), --lines to change how much history is shown, and --follow to stream new entries live until Ctrl+C. This is raw journal text, so there's no -o json mode. homelab-nut logs <host> [flags]
homelab-nut logs pi homelab-nut logs pi --unit nut-server --lines 500 homelab-nut logs pi --follow

-f, --follow stream new entries live until Ctrl+C -h, --help help for logs -n, --lines int number of past journal lines to show (default 200) --unit string systemd unit to read (default "ups-battery-shutdown")
-i, --inventory string path to the inventory YAML file (default: ./homelab-nut.yaml if present, else ~/homelab-nut.yaml)
homelab-nut - Modern CLI + TUI for Network UPS Tools (NUT) Auto generated by spf13/cobra on 24-Jun-2026​
Preview what apply would change on each host (or JSON with -o json)

Loads the inventory, opens SSH connections to each host, runs each role's Detect + Plan, and prints a per-role diff. Read-only — makes no changes on any host. Use the same flags as apply so you can dry-run with one command and execute with another. homelab-nut plan [flags]
-h, --help help for plan -o, --output string output format: text (human) | json (machine-readable for AI/scripts) (default "text")
-i, --inventory string path to the inventory YAML file (default: ./homelab-nut.yaml if present, else ~/homelab-nut.yaml)
homelab-nut - Modern CLI + TUI for Network UPS Tools (NUT) Auto generated by spf13/cobra on 24-Jun-2026​
Commands for the battery-shutdown chain. Today this is just 'test', a dry-run that verifies each target can be reached and its shutdown command resolved — without powering anything off.
-i, --inventory string path to the inventory YAML file (default: ./homelab-nut.yaml if present, else ~/homelab-nut.yaml)

homelab-nut - Modern CLI + TUI for Network UPS Tools (NUT) homelab-nut shutdown test - Dry-run the shutdown chain on every shutdown-target host (no power-off) Auto generated by spf13/cobra on 24-Jun-2026​
Dry-run the shutdown chain on every shutdown-target host (no power-off)

Verifies the shutdown chain works without powering anything off. For each host with the shutdown-target role, this connects over SSH as the configured user and checks that the host's shutdown command can be resolved: a script path (contains '/', e.g. ~/shutdown.sh) must exist and be executable (test -x); an inline command (e.g. poweroff) must be found in PATH (command -v). It prints a host / command / result table. With -o json it emits an array of {host, command, ok, error}. Exits 3 if any host fails the check. homelab-nut shutdown test [flags]
homelab-nut shutdown test homelab-nut shutdown test -o json
-h, --help help for test -o, --output string output format: text (human) | json (machine-readable for AI/scripts) (default "text")
-i, --inventory string path to the inventory YAML file (default: ./homelab-nut.yaml if present, else ~/homelab-nut.yaml)
homelab-nut shutdown - Operate the fleet's shutdown chain Auto generated by spf13/cobra on 24-Jun-2026​
Poll each nut-server host for live UPS state

Connects to every host in the inventory with the nut-server role over the NUT TCP protocol (port 3493) and reports its current UPS state: status string (OL, OB, OB LB, …), battery %, load %, and runtime estimate. Hosts without the nut-server role are skipped (no live UPS state to read). With -o json, output is a JSON array suitable for AI agents and scripts. With --watch, the command redraws every --interval until Ctrl+C; in JSON mode each tick emits a fresh JSON array on its own line (NDJSON-friendly). homelab-nut status [flags]

-h, --help help for status --interval duration redraw cadence when --watch is set (default 5s) -o, --output string output format: text (human) | json (machine-readable for AI/scripts) (default "text") --timeout duration per-host TCP connect + read deadline (default 2s) -w, --watch redraw on an interval instead of exiting (Ctrl+C to stop)
-i, --inventory string path to the inventory YAML file (default: ./homelab-nut.yaml if present, else ~/homelab-nut.yaml)
homelab-nut - Modern CLI + TUI for Network UPS Tools (NUT) Auto generated by spf13/cobra on 24-Jun-2026​
Remove homelab-nut's services + binaries from a host (the inverse of apply)

Reverses what apply installed on a host: stops + removes the custom systemd units (nut-exporter, ups-battery-shutdown), their binaries, the config they wrote, and the shutdown-target sudoers rule + script. The upstream NUT package is left alone unless you pass --purge-nut. Built on the same orchestrator as apply, so single-host and fleet-wide (--all) share one code path, with the same --concurrency and the same --auto-approve / -o json gating. Removals are idempotent — re-running on an already-clean host is a no-op that reports everything as skipped. homelab-nut uninstall [host] [flags]

--all uninstall every host in the inventory (required if no host is given) -y, --auto-approve skip the y/N confirmation prompt before removing --concurrency int max hosts to uninstall in parallel (0 = unlimited) -h, --help help for uninstall -o, --output string output format: text (human) | json (machine-readable for AI/scripts) (default "text") --purge-nut also apt-purge the upstream NUT package and delete /etc/nut (destructive) --role string limit to one role: nut-server|nut-client|exporter|shutdown-daemon|shutdown-target|all (default "all")
-i, --inventory string path to the inventory YAML file (default: ./homelab-nut.yaml if present, else ~/homelab-nut.yaml)
homelab-nut - Modern CLI + TUI for Network UPS Tools (NUT) Auto generated by spf13/cobra on 24-Jun-2026​
Print the version, commit, and build date (or JSON with -o json) homelab-nut version [flags]
-h, --help help for version -o, --output string output format: text (human) | json (machine-readable for AI/scripts) (default "text")
-i, --inventory string path to the inventory YAML file (default: ./homelab-nut.yaml if present, else ~/homelab-nut.yaml)
homelab-nut - Modern CLI + TUI for Network UPS Tools (NUT) Auto generated by spf13/cobra on 24-Jun-2026​
The canonical contributing guide is CONTRIBUTING.md in the repo. This page is a quick summary; please read that one for the full policy.

Improving the NUT setup, client, and exporter scripts New per-host shutdown recipes (UniFi, Synology, TrueNAS, LG TVs, …) The Go CLI + TUI (Cobra subcommands, Bubble Tea screens) The SSH executor + role implementations Better docs, screenshots, examples Notification integrations
Replacing NUT or nut-webgui with a custom implementation Enterprise / HA features Per-distro packaging beyond Debian/Ubuntu (PRs welcome but not a priority)

Test locally. Most setup happens on real machines — there's no CI substitute for running setup-server.sh on a fresh VM or driving the daemon against a real UPS. Run the linters: Go: make lint (golangci-lint) Bash: shellcheck scripts/**/*.sh ups-status.sh Docs site: pnpm --dir apps/docs lint No host-specific values. Use generic placeholders in docs and examples: IPs: 192.0.2.10 (RFC 5737 documentation range) Hosts: myhost, control-node, pi-rack Users: myuser, admin TODOs link to issues. Any TODO or FIXME in code must reference a GitHub issue: // TODO(#42): handle multi-UPS hosts CI (.github/workflows/todo-check.yml) enforces this.
ROADMAP.md — vision, phases, milestones TODOS.md — index of open issues (regenerate with make todos) Issues — grouped by phase-N labels; filter by good first issue if you're new
This project follows the Contributor Covenant. Be kind, assume good intent.

homelab-nut is a CLI + TUI for wiring up Network UPS Tools (NUT) across a homelab — monitor your UPS, push notifications when power events happen, and gracefully shut everything down when the battery runs low. Status The Go CLI is in alpha (v0.1.0-alpha). You can write and inspect an inventory today; SSH-based fleet apply lands in v0.2 (Phase 2). See the Roadmap for status.

Download the binary for your platform from the latest release and unpack it on $PATH: # macOS / Linux — substitute your arch (x86_64, arm64) OS=$(uname -s | tr A-Z a-z) ARCH=$(uname -m); [ "$ARCH" = "aarch64" ] && ARCH=arm64 curl -L "https://github.com/rtorcato/homelab-nut/releases/latest/download/homelab-nut_*_${OS}_${ARCH}.tar.gz" \ | tar -xz homelab-nut sudo install homelab-nut /usr/local/bin/ homelab-nut version Homebrew tap and install.sh are coming in Phase 4.

# Open the interactive TUI (Dashboard, Hosts, Help) homelab-nut # Generate an inventory file interactively homelab-nut init # Inspect what you've defined homelab-nut inventory list homelab-nut inventory show pi-rack homelab-nut inventory validate See the CLI Reference for every subcommand.

The inventory describes the machines in your homelab and what each one does: hosts: - name: pi-rack address: 192.0.2.10 user: pi roles: [nut-server, exporter, shutdown-daemon] ups: name: myups driver: usbhid-ups - name: workstation address: 192.0.2.20 user: admin roles: [nut-client, shutdown-target] shutdown: command: ~/shutdown.sh - name: dream-machine address: 192.0.2.1 user: admin roles: [shutdown-target] shutdown: command: poweroff shutdown_daemon: threshold: 50 poll_interval: 30 slack_webhook_env: SLACK_WEBHOOK Five roles cover the surface: Role What it does nut-server Hosts the UPS via USB/serial, runs nut-server + upsd nut-client Monitors the UPS over the network via upsmon exporter Publishes Prometheus metrics via nut_exporter shutdown-daemon Polls battery state and SSHes targets when threshold is hit shutdown-target Receives a shutdown command — script or inline

The bash scripts in scripts/ remain the working path for actually setting up NUT on machines. The Go CLI today handles inventory definition + inspection; remote setup via homelab-nut apply lands in v0.2. The main README has the current bash-based Quick Start.

The canonical roadmap lives at ROADMAP.md in the repo. Live status of open work is at TODOS.md (regenerated from GitHub Issues with make todos). This page is a thin summary. Read those for the full plan.

Be the tool homelabbers reach for when they need NUT to actually do something. Today, getting NUT to coordinate graceful shutdown across more than one machine is a multi-day project. homelab-nut collapses that to a single command run from your laptop: homelab-nut init # interactive: discover/declare hosts and roles homelab-nut apply # SSH out, install + configure everything homelab-nut status # live UPS dashboard across the fleet homelab-nut # opens a full TUI for ongoing ops Modeled on the tools homelabbers already love — k9s, lazygit, lazydocker, gh, k0sctl, tailscale.

Phase What Milestone 0 — Foundation Issues, labels, project board, CI policy (housekeeping) 1 — Skeleton Go module, Cobra, Bubble Tea TUI, inventory schema, init wizard v0.1 Alpha ✅ 2 — SSH + first apply SSH executor, nut-server role end-to-end v0.1 Alpha 3 — All roles + status Every role wrapped, live status dashboard v0.2 Beta 4 — Polish + distribution Homebrew tap, install.sh, asciinema, this site v1.0 GA 5 — Promotion r/homelab launch, awesome-selfhosted, HN v1.0 GA 6 — Native port Drop bash dependency, multi-distro support post-v1

Language: Go (single static binary, cross-compiles to Linux/macOS/Windows ARM+x86) CLI framework: Cobra TUI framework: Bubble Tea + Lipgloss (Charm) SSH: golang.org/x/crypto/ssh Inventory: YAML homelab-nut.yaml Strategy: wrap existing bash scripts via SSH in v1; progressively port to native Go in Phase 6 Distribution: Homebrew tap, install.sh, goreleaser (deb/rpm/tar.gz)

Replacing NUT itself — we stand on NUT's shoulders Replacing nut-webgui — it does status display well; we orchestrate setup and automation Enterprise / HA cluster support Windows / Mac NUT server support (clients yes, server no) Building our own metrics pipeline — we emit Prometheus via the existing exporter
